  • Overview

    • 2. H2 — Environmental forcing pathways across management systems
    • 3. H3 — Temporal stability and synchrony of causal rules

From pixels to patterns — results and visualisation

Author

Emma Belaud

Overview

Role in the pipeline

This script is the final analytical step of the SEM pipeline. It loads the path coefficient database produced by 3_SEM_modelisation.qmd and addresses the three research questions through figures and tables.

SEM structure

Tier 1 — land use shapes microclimate (additive):

\[\text{microclimate}_1 \leftarrow \beta_1 \cdot \text{land\_use}\] \[\text{microclimate}_2 \leftarrow \beta_2 \cdot \text{land\_use}\]

Tier 2 — (microclimate) × land use drives root:

\[\text{Root} \leftarrow \beta_3 \cdot \text{mc}_1 + \beta_4 \cdot \text{mc}_2 + \beta_5 \cdot \text{lu} + \beta_6 \cdot (\text{mc}_1 \times \text{lu}) + \beta_7 \cdot (\text{mc}_2 \times \text{lu})\]

Tier 3 — (microclimate + root) × land use drives fauna:

\[\text{Fauna} \leftarrow \beta_8 \cdot \text{mc}_1 + \beta_9 \cdot \text{mc}_2 + \beta_{10} \cdot \text{Root} + \beta_{11} \cdot \text{lu} + \beta_{12} \cdot (\text{mc}_1 \times \text{lu}) + \beta_{13} \cdot (\text{mc}_2 \times \text{lu}) + \beta_{14} \cdot (\text{Root} \times \text{lu})\]

where mc = microclimate index, lu = land_use.

The interaction structure is central to the analysis. std_estimate gives the path slope in A (land_use = 0, the unmanaged baseline); std_estimate_C gives the total slope in C (main effect + interaction). p_value tests whether slope A = 0; interaction_p_value tests whether slope C differs from slope A. There is no direct significance test for slope C alone — this asymmetry propagates into all significance summaries reported in figures and tables.

1. H1 — Land-use effects on edaphic conditions and faunal activity

How does land-use management reshape edaphic conditions, and does this translate into contrasted patterns of faunal activity across systems?

This section quantifies the direct effect of land use on each tier of the causal cascade: microclimate first (β₁, β₂), then root dynamics (β₅), then fauna (β₁₁, one per taxon). The slope-graph figure reads as a summary of management impact across the whole system — lines above zero indicate variables that are higher or more active in the managed system, lines below indicate suppression. The seasonal table then unpacks whether these effects are consistent year-round or concentrated in specific phenological periods, which is where disturbance events (irrigation, tillage) would be expected to leave the clearest signature.

Note that β₁₁ is the direct management effect on fauna after conditioning on microclimate and root pathways — it captures the portion of the A vs C contrast that cannot be attributed to altered environmental forcing.

2. H2 — Environmental forcing pathways across management systems

Do microclimate and root dynamics drive fauna through different causal pathways, and does the relative weight of these pathways differ between management systems?

This section examines the five directed paths in the SEM (two microclimate → fauna paths, two microclimate → root paths, and root → fauna) and contrasts their coefficient distributions between A and C. The raincloud plots give an overview of each path’s typical strength and sign across all windows; the Wilcoxon stars indicate whether each distribution is meaningfully shifted from zero. The taxon-level boxplots then test whether system-level contrasts are consistent across taxonomic groups or driven by a subset — a distinction that matters for interpreting whether the management effect is a general community-level signal or taxon-specific.

3. H3 — Temporal stability and synchrony of causal rules

Are the causal links governing faunal activity stable over time, and do both systems respond to environmental cues in a synchronised manner?

Rather than a single point estimate per path, we obtain a time series of β values whose dispersion (CV) measures temporal instability, and whose correlation between A and C (synchrony r) measures how similarly the two systems respond to the same environmental context. Low synchrony on a given path could reflect that management has decoupled that particular causal rule from the shared regional signal — the two systems are experiencing the same climate but translating it into different biological outcomes.

The figure shows the raw temporal trajectories of β; the table summarises instability and synchrony per path and season, allowing seasonal contrasts to be read directly.

Source Code

# Overview

## Role in the pipeline

This script is the **final analytical step** of the SEM pipeline. It loads
the path coefficient database produced by `3_SEM_modelisation.qmd` and
addresses the three research questions through figures and tables.

## SEM structure

**Tier 1 — land use shapes microclimate (additive):**

$$\text{microclimate}_1 \leftarrow \beta_1 \cdot \text{land\_use}$$
$$\text{microclimate}_2 \leftarrow \beta_2 \cdot \text{land\_use}$$

**Tier 2 — (microclimate) × land use drives root:**

$$\text{Root} \leftarrow \beta_3 \cdot \text{mc}_1 + \beta_4 \cdot \text{mc}_2 + \beta_5 \cdot \text{lu} + \beta_6 \cdot (\text{mc}_1 \times \text{lu}) + \beta_7 \cdot (\text{mc}_2 \times \text{lu})$$

**Tier 3 — (microclimate + root) × land use drives fauna:**

$$\text{Fauna} \leftarrow \beta_8 \cdot \text{mc}_1 + \beta_9 \cdot \text{mc}_2 + \beta_{10} \cdot \text{Root} + \beta_{11} \cdot \text{lu} + \beta_{12} \cdot (\text{mc}_1 \times \text{lu}) + \beta_{13} \cdot (\text{mc}_2 \times \text{lu}) + \beta_{14} \cdot (\text{Root} \times \text{lu})$$

*where mc = microclimate index, lu = land\_use.*

![](images/image.png)

The interaction structure is central to the analysis. `std_estimate` gives
the path slope **in A** (land_use = 0, the unmanaged baseline);
`std_estimate_C` gives the total slope **in C** (main effect + interaction).
`p_value` tests whether slope A = 0; `interaction_p_value` tests whether
slope C differs from slope A. There is no direct significance test for
slope C alone — this asymmetry propagates into all significance summaries
reported in figures and tables.

---

```{r}
#| label: setup
invisible(Sys.setlocale("LC_TIME", "C"))
dir.create("../output", showWarnings = FALSE)

libs <- c(
  "tidyverse", "lubridate", "patchwork",
  "kableExtra", "nlme", "zoo", "ggdist", "slider", "ggside"
)
invisible(lapply(libs, require, character.only = TRUE))

# ── Metadata ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
microclimate_index <- readLines("../output/microclimate_index.txt")
fauna_vars         <- readLines("../output/fauna_vars.txt")

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# VISUAL IDENTITY
# All design constants centralised here. GCB target: figures printed at
# 85 mm (single column) or 175 mm (double column) at 300 dpi.
# Font sizes are calibrated for legibility at those print dimensions.
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

SEM_COLORS <- list(
  path = c(
    "#c2cab2",  # mc1 → root  (light sage)
    "#555d45",  # mc2 → root  (dark olive)
    "#dea88b",  # mc1 → fauna (warm sand)
    "#d1855c",  # mc2 → fauna (terracotta)
    "#955f42"   # root → fauna (deep umber)
  ),
  response = c(root = "#94a378", fauna = "#d1855c"),
  position = c(A = "#3d5a3e", C = "#c1603a"),
  sync_low  = "#B2182B",
  sync_mid  = "#F7F7F7",
  sync_high = "#2166AC"
)

position_palette  <- SEM_COLORS$position
response_palette  <- SEM_COLORS$response
position_labeller <- c(A = "Unmanaged (A)", C = "Managed (C)")
season_order      <- c("Spring", "Summer", "Autumn", "Winter")

season_fills <- c(
  Spring = "#F2F2F2",
  Summer = "#E4E4E4",
  Autumn = "#F2F2F2",
  Winter = "#E4E4E4"
)

# ── Path-level ordering ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Ordered from purely abiotic (mc → root) to biotic-mediated (root → fauna),
# reflecting the causal hierarchy and preserved across all figures.
path_levels <- tibble(
  path_order = 1:5,
  path_id = c(
    paste0(microclimate_index[1], " \u2192 root"),
    paste0(microclimate_index[2], " \u2192 root"),
    paste0(microclimate_index[1], " \u2192 fauna"),
    paste0(microclimate_index[2], " \u2192 fauna"),
    "root \u2192 fauna"
  )
)
path_palette <- setNames(SEM_COLORS$path, path_levels$path_id)

# ── Shared ggplot theme ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
theme_sem <- function(base_size = 8, legend_pos = "none") {
  theme_classic(base_size = base_size) %+replace%
    theme(
      panel.border     = element_rect(color = "grey20", fill = NA,
                                      linewidth = 0.3),
      panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey94", linewidth = 0.25),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
      axis.line        = element_blank(),
      axis.ticks       = element_line(color = "grey40", linewidth = 0.3),
      axis.ticks.length = unit(2, "pt"),
      axis.title       = element_text(size = base_size - 0.5, color = "grey20"),
      axis.text        = element_text(size = base_size - 1.5, color = "grey30"),
      strip.text       = element_text(size = base_size - 1, face = "bold",
                                      color = "grey15"),
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey96", color = "grey96",
                                      linewidth = 0.3),
      plot.title    = element_text(size = base_size + 0.5, face = "bold",
                                   hjust = 0, color = "grey10",
                                   margin = margin(b = 3)),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = base_size - 1, color = "grey45",
                                   hjust = 0, margin = margin(b = 5)),
      plot.caption  = element_text(size = base_size - 2, color = "grey55",
                                   hjust = 0, margin = margin(t = 4)),
      legend.position    = legend_pos,
      legend.background  = element_blank(),
      legend.key         = element_blank(),
      legend.title       = element_text(size = base_size - 1, face = "bold",
                                        color = "grey20"),
      legend.text        = element_text(size = base_size - 1.5, color = "grey30"),
      legend.key.size    = unit(8, "pt"),
      legend.spacing.x   = unit(3, "pt"),
      plot.margin = margin(6, 6, 6, 6)
    )
}

# ── Helper: reusable seasonal background layer ────────────────────────────────
make_season_layers <- function(date_range, y_label_pos = Inf, label_size = 2.2) {

  study_years <- seq(year(date_range[1]), year(date_range[2]))

  bands <- tibble(
    season  = names(season_fills),
    m_start = c(3L, 6L, 9L, 12L),
    m_end   = c(6L, 9L, 12L, 3L),
    fill    = unname(season_fills)
  ) %>%
    crossing(year = study_years) %>%
    mutate(
      xmin = as.Date(paste(year, m_start, "01", sep = "-")),
      xmax = as.Date(paste(
        if_else(m_end < m_start, year + 1L, year),
        m_end, "01", sep = "-"
      )),
      xmid = xmin + (xmax - xmin) / 2
    ) %>%
    filter(xmin < date_range[2], xmax > date_range[1]) %>%
    mutate(
      xmin = pmax(xmin, date_range[1]),
      xmax = pmin(xmax, date_range[2])
    )

  rect_layers <- pmap(
    bands %>% select(xmin, xmax, fill),
    function(xmin, xmax, fill)
      annotate("rect", xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax,
               ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
               fill = fill, alpha = 1)
  )

  label_layer <- geom_text(
    data        = bands,
    aes(x = xmid, y = y_label_pos, label = season),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    vjust = 1.6, size = label_size, color = "grey60", fontface = "italic",
    family = "sans"
  )

  c(rect_layers, list(label_layer))
}

# ── Load SEM results database ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
data_sem_results <- read.csv(
  "../output/SEM_results_database.csv",
  na.strings = c("", "NA")
) %>%
  mutate(
    taxon      = if_else(taxon == "fauna", "null model", taxon),
    date_start = as.Date(date_start),
    season     = factor(season, levels = season_order)
  ) %>%
  left_join(path_levels, by = "path_id") %>%
  mutate(
    predictor = case_when(
      predictor == "microclimate_1" ~ microclimate_index[1],
      predictor == "microclimate_2" ~ microclimate_index[2],
      TRUE ~ predictor
    ),
    path_id = str_replace(path_id, "microclimate_1", microclimate_index[1]),
    path_id = str_replace(path_id, "microclimate_2", microclimate_index[2]),
    path_id = factor(path_id, levels = path_levels$path_id)
  )

# ── Load raw observation database ─────────────────────────────────────────────
data_sem_raw <- read.csv("../output/SEM_database.csv",
                         sep = ",", na.strings = c("", "NA")) %>%
  mutate(
    position = as.factor(position),
    depth    = as.factor(depth),
    land_use = as.numeric(position == "C"),
    date     = as.Date(ymd_hms(date, tz = "UTC")),
    root     = root_growth_cm2_count_24h,
    month    = month(date),
    season   = factor(case_when(
      month %in% c(3,  4,  5) ~ "Spring",
      month %in% c(6,  7,  8) ~ "Summer",
      month %in% c(9, 10, 11) ~ "Autumn",
      TRUE                    ~ "Winter"
    ), levels = season_order)
  )

# ── Reshape SEM results: wide → long A / C ────────────────────────────────────
# std_estimate   = β in A (land_use = 0, baseline)
# std_estimate_C = β in C (main effect + interaction combined)
data_long <- data_sem_results %>%
  filter(!is.na(path_id)) %>%
  select(taxon, date_start, path_id, predictor, response,
         season, month, path_order, r2_cond, r2_marg,
         std_estimate, std_estimate_C,
         p_value, interaction_p_value, has_interaction) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(std_estimate, std_estimate_C),
    names_to  = "position",
    values_to = "beta"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    position = if_else(position == "std_estimate", "A", "C"),
    position = factor(position, levels = c("A", "C"))
  )

# ── Wide format for direct A vs C comparison ──────────────────────────────────
beta_wide <- data_long %>%
  select(taxon, date_start, path_id, position, beta,
         interaction_p_value, p_value, season) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from  = position,
    values_from = beta,
    values_fn   = list(beta = first)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(A), !is.na(C)) %>%
  mutate(sig_diff = !is.na(interaction_p_value) & interaction_p_value < 0.05)

# ── Outlier filter for distribution plots ─────────────────────────────────────
# Applied only to visualisation; tables use the full distribution.
Q1_val  <- quantile(data_long$beta, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)
Q3_val  <- quantile(data_long$beta, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
IQR_val <- Q3_val - Q1_val

data_plot <- data_long %>%
  filter(
    beta >= Q1_val - 1.5 * IQR_val,
    beta <= Q3_val + 1.5 * IQR_val,
    !is.na(path_id)
  )
```

---

## 1. H1 — Land-use effects on edaphic conditions and faunal activity

> **How does land-use management reshape edaphic conditions, and does this
> translate into contrasted patterns of faunal activity across systems?**

This section quantifies the direct effect of land use on each tier of the
causal cascade: microclimate first (β₁, β₂), then root dynamics (β₅), then
fauna (β₁₁, one per taxon). The slope-graph figure reads as a summary of
management impact across the whole system — lines above zero indicate
variables that are higher or more active in the managed system, lines below
indicate suppression. The seasonal table then unpacks whether these effects
are consistent year-round or concentrated in specific phenological periods,
which is where disturbance events (irrigation, tillage) would be expected to
leave the clearest signature.

Note that β₁₁ is the *direct* management effect on fauna after conditioning
on microclimate and root pathways — it captures the portion of the A vs C
contrast that cannot be attributed to altered environmental forcing.

```{r landuse-shared}
#| label: landuse-shared

# ── Shared data: computed once, used by both figure and table ─────────────────
# β₁, β₂ : land_use → microclimate (Tier 1) — from all_taxa run only,
#           as these equations are independent of the fauna variable modelled.
# β₅     : land_use → root (Tier 2) — same rationale.
# β₁₁    : land_use → fauna (Tier 3) — retained per taxon.

env_raw <- data_sem_results %>%
  filter(
    predictor == "land_use",
    response  %in% c("microclimate_1", "microclimate_2", "root")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    label = case_when(
      response == "microclimate_1" ~ paste0("\u03b2\u2081 (", microclimate_index[1], ")"),
      response == "microclimate_2" ~ paste0("\u03b2\u2082 (", microclimate_index[2], ")"),
      response == "root"           ~ "\u03b2\u2085 (root growth)"
    ),
    group = case_when(
      response %in% c("microclimate_1", "microclimate_2") ~ "Microclimate",
      response == "root"                                  ~ "Root growth"
    ),
    season = factor(season, levels = season_order)
  )

fauna_raw <- data_sem_results %>%
  filter(predictor == "land_use", response == "fauna") %>%
  mutate(
    label  = if_else(taxon == "null model", "all_taxa", taxon),
    group  = "Faunal activity",
    season = factor(season, levels = season_order)
  )

all_raw <- bind_rows(
  env_raw   %>% select(label, group, season, std_estimate, p_value, date_start),
  fauna_raw %>% select(label, group, season, std_estimate, p_value, date_start)
)

all_global <- all_raw %>%
  group_by(label, group) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_b  = mean(std_estimate, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_b    = sd(std_estimate,   na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_sig = mean(p_value < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    sig_label = paste0(round(pct_sig, 0), "% sig."),
    direction = if_else(mean_b > 0, "#c1603a", "#3d5a3e"),
    linetype  = case_when(
      group == "Microclimate"    ~ "dotted",
      group == "Root growth"     ~ "dashed",
      group == "Faunal activity" ~ "solid"
    )
  ) %>%
  mutate(group = factor(group,
                        levels = c("Microclimate", "Root growth",
                                   "Faunal activity"))) %>%
  arrange(group, mean_b) %>%
  mutate(label = fct_inorder(label))

mc_labels    <- all_global %>% filter(group == "Microclimate")    %>% pull(label) %>% as.character()
root_labels  <- all_global %>% filter(group == "Root growth")     %>% pull(label) %>% as.character()
fauna_labels <- all_global %>% filter(group == "Faunal activity") %>% pull(label) %>% as.character()
fauna_labels_ordered <- c("all_taxa", setdiff(fauna_labels, "all_taxa"))
table_levels   <- c(mc_labels, root_labels, fauna_labels_ordered)
edaphic_labels <- c(mc_labels, root_labels, "all_taxa")

all_season <- all_raw %>%
  group_by(label, group, season) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_b  = mean(std_estimate, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_b    = sd(std_estimate,   na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_sig = mean(p_value < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    b_label   = paste0(round(mean_b, 2), " \u00b1 ", round(sd_b, 2)),
    sig_label = paste0(round(pct_sig, 0), "%")
  )

label_colors <- deframe(select(all_global, label, direction))
```

```{r fig-landuse}
#| label: fig-landuse
#| fig-cap: >
#|   Direct effect of land-use management on edaphic conditions and faunal
#|   activity (standardised β per variable or taxon, averaged across all rolling
#|   windows). Each line connects the unmanaged baseline (A = 0 by definition)
#|   to the mean coefficient in the managed system (C); 
#|   Line type encodes the response tier: dotted = microclimate
#|   (β₁, β₂); dashed = root growth (β₅); solid = faunal taxa (β₁₁).
#|   Labels report the proportion of windows where the effect was significant
#|   (p < 0.05). Terracotta = increased under management; olive = reduced.
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 6

p_landuse <- ggplot() +

  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "grey30", linewidth = 0.25,
             linetype = "dashed") +

  geom_segment(
    data = all_global,
    aes(x = 0, xend = 1, y = 0, yend = mean_b,
        color    = label,
        linetype = linetype),
    linewidth = 0.55, lineend = "round",
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +

  geom_point(data = all_global,
             aes(x = 0, y = 0,      color = label), size = 1.1, shape = 16) +
  geom_point(data = all_global,
             aes(x = 1, y = mean_b, color = label), size = 1.1, shape = 16) +

  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = all_global,
    aes(x = 1, y = mean_b, color = label,
        label    = paste0(label, "  ", sig_label),
        fontface = if_else(label %in% edaphic_labels, "bold", "plain")),
    nudge_x = 0.08, hjust = 0, size = 2.1,
    segment.size = 0.2, segment.color = "grey70",
    direction = "y", box.padding = 0.12,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +

  scale_color_manual(values = label_colors, guide = "none") +
  scale_linetype_identity() +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = c(0, 1),
    labels = c("Unmanaged (A)", "Managed (C)"),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.12, 0.32))
  ) +
  labs(x = NULL,
       y = "Direct land-use effect (standardised \u03b2)") +
  theme_sem() +
  theme(panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x       = element_blank())

p_landuse

tiff("../output/Fig1b_landuse_effects.tiff",
     width = 12, height = 14, units = "cm", res = 300)
print(p_landuse)
invisible(dev.off())
```

```{r tbl-landuse}
#| label: tbl-landuse
#| tbl-cap: >
#|   Seasonal breakdown of direct land-use effects (mean β ± SD across rolling
#|   windows) on microclimate (β₁, β₂), root growth (β₅), and faunal activity
#|   per taxon (β₁₁). % sig = proportion of windows with p < 0.05.
#|   Terracotta = increased under management; olive = reduced.

all_wide <- all_season %>%
  select(label, group, season, b_label, sig_label) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from  = season,
              values_from = c(b_label, sig_label),
              names_glue  = "{season}_{.value}") %>%
  mutate(label = factor(label, levels = table_levels)) %>%
  arrange(label)

col_order <- as.vector(rbind(paste0(season_order, "_b_label"),
                             paste0(season_order, "_sig_label")))

display_tbl <- all_wide %>%
  select(Label = label, all_of(col_order))

col_names     <- c("Variable / Taxon",
                   rep(c("Mean \u03b2 \u00b1 SD", "% sig."),
                       length(season_order)))
bold_rows     <- which(all_wide$label %in% edaphic_labels)
b_positions   <- which(str_detect(names(display_tbl), "_b_label$"))
sig_positions <- which(str_detect(names(display_tbl), "_sig_label$"))

n_mc    <- length(mc_labels)
n_root  <- length(root_labels)
n_fauna <- nrow(all_wide) - n_mc - n_root

all_means <- all_season %>%
  select(label, season, mean_b) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from  = season,
              values_from = mean_b,
              names_glue  = "{season}_mean_b") %>%
  mutate(label = factor(label, levels = table_levels)) %>%
  arrange(label)

tbl <- display_tbl %>%
  kable(col.names = col_names) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("hover", "condensed"),
                full_width = TRUE, font_size = 11) %>%
  add_header_above(setNames(c(1L, rep(2L, length(season_order))),
                            c(" ", season_order)),
                   bold = TRUE, background = "#ebebeb") %>%
  row_spec(0,         bold = TRUE, background = "#d8d8d8") %>%
  row_spec(bold_rows, bold = TRUE) %>%
  pack_rows("Microclimate (\u03b2\u2081, \u03b2\u2082)",
            1, n_mc,
            label_row_css = "background-color:#e8e8e8; font-weight:bold;") %>%
  pack_rows("Root growth (\u03b2\u2085)",
            n_mc + 1, n_mc + n_root,
            label_row_css = "background-color:#eeeeee; font-weight:bold;") %>%
  pack_rows("Faunal activity (\u03b2\u2081\u2081)",
            n_mc + n_root + 1, n_mc + n_root + n_fauna,
            label_row_css = "background-color:#f4f4f4; font-weight:bold;") %>%
  reduce(seq_along(b_positions), function(t, i) {
    vals <- all_means[[paste0(season_order[i], "_mean_b")]]
    column_spec(t, b_positions[i], bold = TRUE,
                color = if_else(vals > 0, "#c1603a",
                        if_else(vals < 0, "#3d5a3e", "grey55")))
  }, .init = .)

tbl <- reduce(sig_positions, function(t, col_i) {
  column_spec(t, col_i, italic = TRUE, color = "grey45")
}, .init = tbl)

tbl
```

---

## 2. H2 — Environmental forcing pathways across management systems

> **Do microclimate and root dynamics drive fauna through different causal
> pathways, and does the relative weight of these pathways differ between
> management systems?**

This section examines the five directed paths in the SEM (two microclimate →
fauna paths, two microclimate → root paths, and root → fauna) and contrasts
their coefficient distributions between A and C. The raincloud plots give an
overview of each path's typical strength and sign across all windows; the
Wilcoxon stars indicate whether each distribution is meaningfully shifted
from zero. The taxon-level boxplots then test whether system-level contrasts
are consistent across taxonomic groups or driven by a subset — a distinction
that matters for interpreting whether the management effect is a general
community-level signal or taxon-specific.

```{r}
#| label: prep-sig-summary

# Significance rates computed separately per system:
#   A: p_value tests slope A ≠ 0
#   C: interaction_p_value tests slope C ≠ slope A
# These are different hypotheses — the asymmetry is noted in captions.
sig_summary <- data_long %>%
  filter(!is.na(path_id)) %>%
  group_by(path_id, position) %>%
  summarise(
    n_total  = n(),
    n_sig    = if_else(
      first(position) == "A",
      sum(p_value < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
      sum(!is.na(interaction_p_value) & interaction_p_value < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)
    ),
    prop_sig = n_sig / n_total * 100,
    .groups  = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(sig_label = paste0("Sig. rate: \n", round(prop_sig, 0), "%"))

sig_stars <- function(p) {
  case_when(
    p < 0.001 ~ "***",
    p < 0.01  ~ "**",
    p < 0.05  ~ "*",
    TRUE      ~ "ns"
  )
}

# One-sample Wilcoxon test: is β distribution shifted from zero?
# Robust to heavy tails typical of rolling-window series.
wilcox_path <- data_plot %>%
  filter(!is.na(beta)) %>%
  group_by(path_id, position) %>%
  summarise(
    p_wilcox    = tryCatch(
      wilcox.test(beta, mu = 0, exact = FALSE)$p.value,
      error = function(e) NA_real_
    ),
    median_beta = median(beta, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(stars  = sig_stars(p_wilcox),
         y_star = Inf)

wilcox_taxa <- data_plot %>%
  filter(!is.na(beta), taxon != "all_taxa", response == "fauna") %>%
  group_by(path_id, position, taxon) %>%
  summarise(
    p_wilcox    = tryCatch(
      wilcox.test(beta, mu = 0, exact = FALSE)$p.value,
      error = function(e) NA_real_
    ),
    median_beta = median(beta, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(stars = sig_stars(p_wilcox))
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-path-distributions
#| fig-cap: >
#|   Distributions of standardised path coefficients (β) per causal path and
#|   management system across all rolling windows. The shaded half-eye shows
#|   the kernel density; the box spans the IQR with a median line; dots below
#|   show individual window estimates (hex layout). Extreme values (beyond
#|   Q1 ± 1.5×IQR) excluded. Top-right: proportion of windows where the path
#|   was significant (A: main effect p < 0.05; C: interaction p < 0.05 —
#|   note these test different hypotheses). Bottom-right: Wilcoxon signed-rank
#|   test against zero (*** p < 0.001, ** p < 0.01, * p < 0.05, ns).
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 5

p_rain <- data_plot %>%
  ggplot(aes(beta, fill = path_id, color = path_id)) +

  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "grey30", linewidth = 0.35,
             linetype = "dashed") +

  stat_halfeye(
    aes(fill = path_id),
    adjust = 1.2, width = 0.55, justification = -0.08,
    .width = 0, point_colour = NA, alpha = 0.75
  ) +

  geom_boxplot(
    width = 0.13, outliers = FALSE, linewidth = 0.3,
    color = "grey20", fill = "white", alpha = 0.6
  ) +

  stat_dots(
    side = "bottom", justification = 1.08,
    binwidth = unit(c(0.002, 0.015), "npc"),
    color = NA, alpha = 0.55, layout = "hex", dotsize = 0.8
  ) +

  geom_text(
    data        = sig_summary,
    aes(x = Inf, y = Inf, label = sig_label),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    hjust = 1.12, vjust = 1.3, size = 2.6,
    fontface = "italic", color = "grey35"
  ) +

  geom_text(
    data        = wilcox_path,
    aes(x = Inf, y = -Inf, label = stars),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    hjust = 1.15, vjust = -0.6,
    size = 3.2, fontface = "bold",
    color = if_else(wilcox_path$stars == "ns", "grey55", "grey15")
  ) +

  scale_fill_manual(values  = path_palette) +
  scale_color_manual(values = path_palette) +
  guides(fill = "none", color = "none") +

  facet_grid(
    position ~ path_id,
    labeller = labeller(
      position = position_labeller,
      path_id  = label_wrap_gen(16)
    )
  ) +

  labs(
    x = expression("Standardised path coefficient"~(beta)),
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_sem() +
  theme(
    panel.spacing.x = unit(4, "pt"),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(6, "pt"),
    axis.text.y     = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y    = element_blank()
  )

p_rain

tiff("../output/Fig2_path_distributions.tiff",
     width = 15, height = 13, units = "cm", res = 300)
print(p_rain)
invisible(dev.off())
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-path-by-taxon
#| fig-cap: >
#|   Path coefficients stratified by taxonomic group and causal path. Each
#|   panel shows the IQR (box) and full range (whiskers) across rolling windows.
#|   Coloured by causal path; A = outline only (alpha 0.2), C = filled
#|   (alpha 0.75). Stars: Wilcoxon test of β vs 0 per position × taxon
#|   (*** p < 0.001, ** p < 0.01, * p < 0.05, ns). Consistent patterns
#|   across taxa support a community-level interpretation; divergent patterns
#|   indicate taxon-specific responses.
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 5

p_taxa <- data_plot %>%
  filter(taxon != "all_taxa", response == "fauna") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = position, y = beta)) +

  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "grey30", linewidth = 0.3,
             linetype = "dashed") +

  geom_boxplot(
    aes(fill = path_id, alpha = position),
    outliers  = FALSE,
    linewidth = 0.1,
    color     = "grey25",
    width     = 0.9
  ) +

  geom_text(
    data        = wilcox_taxa,
    aes(x = position, y = Inf, label = stars),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    vjust = 1.4, hjust = 0.3, size = 2, fontface = "bold",
    color = if_else(wilcox_taxa$stars == "ns", "grey65", "grey15")
  ) +

  scale_fill_manual(values = path_palette, guide = "none") +
  scale_alpha_manual(
    values = c(A = 0.2, C = 0.75),
    labels = position_labeller,
    name   = "System"
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c(A = "A", C = "C")) +

  facet_grid(
    path_id  ~ taxon,
    scales   = "free_y",
    labeller = labeller(path_id = label_wrap_gen(16))
  ) +

  labs(
    x = "Management system",
    y = expression("Standardised path coefficient"~(beta))
  ) +
  guides(fill = "none", alpha = "none") +
  theme_sem(legend_pos = "bottom") +
  theme(
    panel.spacing.x = unit(2, "pt"),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(2, "pt"),
    axis.text.x     = element_text(size = 7.5)
  )

p_taxa

tiff("../output/Fig2b_path_by_taxon.tiff",
     width = 15, height = 13, units = "cm", res = 300)
print(p_taxa)
invisible(dev.off())
```

---

## 3. H3 — Temporal stability and synchrony of causal rules

> **Are the causal links governing faunal activity stable over time, and do
> both systems respond to environmental cues in a synchronised manner?**

Rather than a single point estimate per path, we obtain a time series of β values whose dispersion (CV) measures temporal instability, and whose correlation between A and C (synchrony r) measures how similarly the two systems respond to the same
environmental context. Low synchrony on a given path could reflect that management has decoupled that particular causal rule from the shared regional signal — the two systems are experiencing the same climate but translating it into different biological outcomes.

The figure shows the raw temporal trajectories of β; the table summarises
instability and synchrony per path and season, allowing seasonal contrasts
to be read directly.

```{r}
#| label: fig-temporal-paths
#| fig-cap: >
#|   Temporal dynamics of standardised path coefficients (β) per causal path
#|   across the study period. Semi-transparent points = individual window
#|   estimates; lines = LOESS trends (span = 0.25); ribbons = 95% pointwise
#|   confidence intervals. The light grey band marks |β| < 0.2 (approximate
#|   null zone). Seasonal backgrounds as in Fig. 1. Wide ribbons indicate
#|   high temporal variability; diverging A/C trends indicate management-driven
#|   decoupling of that causal rule.
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 5

date_range_sem <- range(data_plot$date_start, na.rm = TRUE)

x_breaks_sem <- seq(
  floor_date(date_range_sem[1], "month"),
  ceiling_date(date_range_sem[2], "month"),
  by = "3 months"
)

p_temporal <- ggplot() +

  make_season_layers(date_range_sem, label_size = 2.0) +

  annotate("rect",
           xmin = -Inf, xmax = Inf, ymin = -0.2, ymax = 0.2,
           fill = "grey50", alpha = 0.08) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "grey30", linewidth = 0.3,
             linetype = "solid") +

  geom_point(
    data  = data_plot,
    aes(date_start, beta, color = position),
    alpha = 0.18, size = 0.15, shape = 16
  ) +

  geom_smooth(
    data      = data_plot,
    aes(date_start, beta, color = position, fill = position),
    method    = "loess", span = 0.25,
    se        = TRUE, linewidth = 0.7, alpha = 0.12
  ) +

  scale_color_manual(values = position_palette, labels = position_labeller,
                     name = NULL) +
  scale_fill_manual(values  = position_palette, labels = position_labeller,
                    name = NULL) +
  scale_x_date(
    breaks      = x_breaks_sem,
    date_labels = "%b\n%y",
    expand      = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.01))
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(-1.1, 1.1),
    breaks = seq(-1, 1, 0.5)
  ) +

  facet_wrap(
    ~ path_id,
    ncol     = 2,
    labeller = labeller(path_id = label_wrap_gen(22))
  ) +

  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = expression("Standardised path coefficient"~(beta))
  ) +
  theme_sem(legend_pos = "bottom") +
  theme(
    panel.spacing.x   = unit(10, "pt"),
    panel.spacing.y   = unit(8, "pt"),
    axis.text.x       = element_text(size = 7.5, lineheight = 0.85,
                                     hjust = 0.5),
    legend.direction  = "horizontal"
  )

p_temporal

tiff("../output/Fig3_temporal_dynamics.tiff",
     width = 15, height = 13, units = "cm", res = 300)
print(p_temporal)
invisible(dev.off())
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-cv-beta
#| tbl-cap: >
#|   Temporal instability and cross-system synchrony of path coefficients per
#|   causal path and period. Mean β ± SD characterises typical effect size and
#|   dispersion within each system. Δ CV = CV(C) − CV(A) in percentage points:
#|   positive values indicate greater temporal instability of that causal rule
#|   under management. Synchrony r: Pearson correlation between the β_A and
#|   β_C time series within each period; r ≈ 1 = both systems respond to the
#|   same environmental timing; r ≈ 0 = management-driven decoupling.

seasonal_stats <- data_long %>%
  filter(!is.na(path_id), !is.na(beta)) %>%
  group_by(path_id, season) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_A  = mean(beta[position == "A"], na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_A    = sd(beta[position == "A"],   na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_C  = mean(beta[position == "C"], na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_C    = sd(beta[position == "C"],   na.rm = TRUE),
    cv_A    = (sd_A  / abs(mean_A)) * 100,
    cv_C    = (sd_C  / abs(mean_C)) * 100,
    r_sync  = cor(
      beta[position == "A"],
      beta[position == "C"],
      use = "pairwise.complete.obs"
    ),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(delta_cv = cv_C - cv_A)

global_stats <- data_long %>%
  filter(!is.na(path_id), !is.na(beta)) %>%
  group_by(path_id) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_A  = mean(beta[position == "A"], na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_A    = sd(beta[position == "A"],   na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_C  = mean(beta[position == "C"], na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_C    = sd(beta[position == "C"],   na.rm = TRUE),
    cv_A    = (sd_A  / abs(mean_A)) * 100,
    cv_C    = (sd_C  / abs(mean_C)) * 100,
    r_sync  = cor(
      beta[position == "A"],
      beta[position == "C"],
      use = "pairwise.complete.obs"
    ),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(delta_cv = cv_C - cv_A, season = "Whole period")

table_long <- bind_rows(global_stats, seasonal_stats) %>%
  mutate(
    season   = factor(season, levels = c("Whole period", season_order)),
    label_A  = paste0(round(mean_A, 2), " \u00b1 ", round(sd_A, 2)),
    label_C  = paste0(round(mean_C, 2), " \u00b1 ", round(sd_C, 2)),
    label_dc = as.character(round(delta_cv, 1)),
    label_r  = as.character(round(r_sync,   2))
  )

table_display <- table_long %>%
  select(path_id, season, label_A, label_C, label_dc, label_r) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(label_A, label_C, label_dc, label_r),
               names_to = "metric") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = path_id, values_from = value) %>%
  arrange(season) %>%
  select(-season) %>%
  mutate(Metric = case_when(
    metric == "label_A"  ~ "\u03b2 unmanaged A (mean \u00b1 SD)",
    metric == "label_C"  ~ "\u03b2 managed C (mean \u00b1 SD)",
    metric == "label_dc" ~ "\u0394 CV (C \u2212 A, pp)",
    metric == "label_r"  ~ "Synchrony (r)",
    TRUE                 ~ metric
  )) %>%
  select(Metric, everything(), -metric)

delta_rows     <- seq(3, nrow(table_display), by = 4)
sync_rows      <- seq(4, nrow(table_display), by = 4)
path_col_indices <- 2:ncol(table_display)

tbl <- table_display %>%
  kable(booktabs = TRUE) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    font_size = 11, full_width = TRUE
  ) %>%
  pack_rows("Whole period", 1, 4,
            label_row_css = "background-color:#e8e8e8; font-weight:bold;") %>%
  pack_rows("Spring",  5,  8, label_row_css = "background-color:#f4f4f4;") %>%
  pack_rows("Summer",  9, 12, label_row_css = "background-color:#f4f4f4;") %>%
  pack_rows("Autumn", 13, 16, label_row_css = "background-color:#f4f4f4;") %>%
  pack_rows("Winter", 17, 20, label_row_css = "background-color:#f4f4f4;") %>%
  column_spec(1, bold = TRUE, width = "16em") %>%
  row_spec(sync_rows, color = "#2166AC", italic = TRUE)

reduce(path_col_indices, function(t, col_i) {
  col_values  <- table_display[[col_i]]
  cell_colors <- map_chr(1:nrow(table_display), function(row_j) {
    if (row_j %in% delta_rows) {
      val <- as.numeric(col_values[row_j])
      if (is.na(val))  return("black")
      if (val > 0)     return("#c1603a")
      if (val < 0)     return("#3d5a3e")
      return("grey55")
    } else {
      return("black")
    }
  })
  column_spec(t, col_i,
              color = cell_colors,
              bold  = if_else(1:nrow(table_display) %in% delta_rows, TRUE, FALSE))
}, .init = tbl)
```
